# Code of Conduct

## Our pledge

We pledge to make participation in Tura free from harassment for everyone,
regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics,
gender identity and expression, experience level, education, socioeconomic
status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, or
sexual identity and orientation.

We commit to acting in ways that support an open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive,
and healthy community.

## Project values

We believe the best agent harness should be open source. Inspectable prompts,
tool contracts, runners, benchmarks, evaluation criteria, and failure evidence
make technical claims accountable and allow the community to improve them.
Disagreement about implementations or measurements is welcome when it is
evidence-based and respectful. Concealing benchmark-only behavior, manipulating
results, or attacking people who question a claim is not consistent with this
community.

## Expected behavior

- Be respectful, specific, and constructive.
- Critique ideas, code, measurements, and behavior rather than people.
- State uncertainty and correct mistakes without hostility.
- Respect privacy, boundaries, and differing levels of experience.
- Accept responsibility, apologize when appropriate, and repair harm.
- Focus on what is best for the project and community.

## Unacceptable behavior

- Sexualized language, imagery, or unwanted attention.
- Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks.
- Public or private harassment, threats, stalking, or intimidation.
- Publishing another person's private information without explicit permission.
- Deliberate disruption, bad-faith benchmark manipulation, or retaliation after
  a report.
- Conduct that would reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional
  setting.

## Scope

This code applies in project spaces and when an individual officially represents
Tura in public spaces. Project spaces include repositories, issue trackers,
review threads, community channels, events, and private project communication.

## Reporting and enforcement

Report conduct concerns privately to `info@turaai.net`. Include relevant links,
dates, context, and any immediate safety concern. Do not publish sensitive
details in an issue.

Maintainers will review reports promptly, protect reporter privacy as far as
reasonably possible, avoid conflicts of interest, and take proportionate action.
Possible actions include a private correction, warning, temporary restriction,
removal of content, temporary ban, or permanent ban. Retaliation is prohibited.

Anyone asked to stop unacceptable behavior is expected to comply immediately.
If the primary maintainer is involved in a report, use the project contact and
request review by an uninvolved maintainer.

## Attribution

This policy is adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1:
<https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html>.
